name: Sailor's Watch Cap
description: A dark knitted wool cap worn low for warmth, suited to sailors standing watch in cold Atlantic winds and harbor weather.

name: Traveler's Broadbrim Hat
description: A weathered brown felt hat with a broad brim, practical for travelers, riders, and overland journeys between coast and frontier.

name: Naval Officer's Tricorn
description: A navy tricorn trimmed in gold, fit for an officer tied to Atlantic trade, imperial fleets, or ceremonial naval service.

name: Meetinghouse Broadbrim
description: A simple dark felt broadbrim with a tall crown and plain band, reflecting the restrained dress favored in many colonial communities.

name: Surveyor's Round Hat
description: A sturdy round felt hat with a leather band, suitable for surveyors, mapmakers, and practical men crossing fields and wooded tracts.

name: Explorer's Fur Cap
description: A fur-lined cap with leather reinforcing bands, useful for cold-weather expeditions, northern routes, and rugged exploration.

name: Woven Plantation Hat
description: A wide woven straw hat for shade in warmer climates, suited to field labor, plantation work, and long days under southern sun.

name: Courtier's Velvet Cap
description: A richly embroidered velvet cap with gold ornamentation, suggesting the display of status and luxury within elite Atlantic society.

name: Weathered Tricorn
description: A worn dark tricorn with brass edging, the sort of reliable hat a colonial traveler, militia member, or dockside worker might wear.

name: Dark Felt Cavalier Hat
description: A flat-crowned black felt hat with a wide brim, offering a bold silhouette reminiscent of older Atlantic fashions carried into the colonies.
